title: allium subhirsutum subsp. graecum
intro: Allium subhirsutum subsp. graecum, also known as Wild Garlic, is a subspecies of the Allium subhirsutum plant, a perennial bulbous plant native to the Mediterranean region. It is characterized by its strong onion-like flavor and aroma, and is used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include antibacterial, antifungal, and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of Allium subhirsutum subsp. graecum include reducing symptoms of respiratory infections, such as colds and flu, and its antimicrobial properties make it effective against various types of infections. In herbal preparations, it is often used as a natural remedy in teas, infusions, and salves, and is also used as a natural preservative in food and cosmetics.
